channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Holocaust survivor recalls mother's encounter with Nazi
item.description: Marianne Muller joins “NewsNation Prime” to share her family's story of courage and survival during the Holocaust.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: Belarus strongman extends his rule after an election rejected by opposition and the EU
item.description: Belarus' authoritarian President Alexander Lukashenko extended his more than three decades in power in Sunday’s orchestrated election that the opposition and the European Union rejected as a sham.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Korean plane crash report says bird remains were found in engines, but no cause yet revealed
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The first report on last month’s Jeju Air crash in South Korea confirmed traces of bird strikes in the plane's engines, though officials haven’t determined the cause of the accident that killed all but two of the 181 people on board.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Takeaways from AP's reporting on the thousands disappeared in Colombia, Peru and Paraguay
item.description: Thousands of people have disappeared in Latin America during decadeslong conflicts. Many have never been found, presumed to be the victims of dictatorships, insurgencies or organized crime.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tens of thousands return to devastated northern Gaza as Israel lifts its closure under truce
item.description: Tens of thousands of Palestinians are streaming into the most heavily destroyed part of the Gaza Strip after Israel lifted its closure of the north for the first time since the early weeks of the 15-month war with Hamas.
item.dc:creator: By Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy And Joseph Krauss, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Deal reached to release Israeli hostage and allow Palestinians into north Gaza
item.description: Mediator Qatar announced early Monday that an agreement has been reached to release an Israeli civilian hostage and allow Palestinians to return to northern Gaza, easing the first major crisis of the fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Moreno says he will introduce legislation to sanction Colombia
item.description: Sen. Bernie Moreno (R-Ohio) said Sunday he will introduce legislation to sanction Colombia this week after the country’s president reportedly denied entry to two planes carrying deported migrants from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Colombia to send presidential plane to Honduras to pick up migrants from US flights
item.description: President Gustavo Petro has arranged for the presidential plane to facilitate the "dignified return of Colombian nationals "
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: Musk tells Germans to get over 'past guilt' at far-right AfD rally
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk told Germans they should be proud of their heritage and should “move beyond” the “past guilt” of older generations.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: 13 UN peacekeepers, allied soldiers dead in Congo as M23 rebels make gains in key city
item.description: The Rwanda-backed rebel group M23 is battling Congolese government forces in Congo's key city of Goma, causing the international airport there to be evacuated.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sweden seizes vessel suspected of 'sabotage' after undersea data cable rupture in Baltic Sea
item.description: Sweden has opened a preliminary investigation into suspected aggravated “sabotage” and ordered the detention of a vessel in the Baltic Sea suspected of damaging an underwater fiber optic cable connecting Latvia and the Swedish island of Gotland earlier that day.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Colombia’s president says he will deny entry of US deportation flights
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro said he will deny entry to the United States's deportation flights as President Trump's immigration plan begins. “The US cannot treat Colombian migrants as criminals,” Petro posted Sunday to the social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Paraguay's disappeared: A dictator's shadow is a roadblock for justice, but a few keep up the fight
item.description: Paraguayans’ mission to search for loved ones feels less visible and more challenging than others in Latin America.
item.dc:creator: María Teresa Hernández, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump wants Egypt and Jordan to take in Palestinians from Gaza. Here's why they are likely to refuse
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's suggestion that Egypt and Jordan take in Palestinians from the war-ravaged Gaza Strip is likely to be met with a hard “no" from the two U.S. allies and the Palestinians themselves, who fear Israel would never allow them to return.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: CIA believes COVID most likely originated from lab but has low confidence in its own finding
item.description: The CIA now believes the virus responsible for the COVID-19 pandemic most likely originated from a laboratory, according to an assessment released Saturday that points the finger at China even while acknowledging that the spy agency has “low confidence” in its own conclusion.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Georgia's leader calls for Trump's 'strong America' to push back Russia
item.description: Salome Zourabichvili, Georgia's fifth president, is on a mission in Washington to make her country matter to President Trump and his incoming team’s "America First" foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump's world leaders club: who’s in and who's looking to salvage ties
item.description: President Donald Trump's second term is already bringing massive changes as global leaders, allies and adversaries alike, watch to see where they fall in the pecking order and vie for a seat at the table.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: International peacekeepers killed as fighting rages around eastern Congo's key city
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Fighting with M23 rebels in eastern Congo has left at least 13 peacekeepers and foreign soldiers dead, United Nations and army officials said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea says it tested cruise missile system and vows 'toughest' response to US
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea said Sunday it tested a cruise missile system, its third known weapons display this year, and vowed “the toughest” response to what it called the escalation of U.S.-South Korean military drills that target the North.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas frees 4 female Israeli soldiers in exchange for 200 Palestinian prisoners as ceasefire holds
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Four female Israeli soldiers who were taken in the attack that sparked the war in Gaza returned to Israel on Saturday after Hamas militants paraded them before a crowd of thousands in Gaza City and handed them over to the Red Cross.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, SAM MEDNICK, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Opus Dei cardinal acknowledges Vatican sanctioned him after abuse allegation but denies wrongdoing
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — The once-powerful archbishop of Lima, Peru and the first-ever cardinal of Opus Dei acknowledged Saturday that the Vatican had imposed sanctions on him in 2019 following an allegation of sexual abuse, but he strongly denied any wrongdoing.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colombia president decrees emergency powers to restore order in coca region wracked by rebel combat
item.description: Colombia's President Gustavo Petro has issued a decree giving him emergency powers to restore order in Catatumbo, a coca-growing region wracked by a deadly turf war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A 2,000-year-old statue is found abandoned in a garbage bag in Greece
item.description: A marble statue believed to be more than 2,000 years old has been found in a garbage bag near the city of Thessaloniki in Greece; experts say the piece dates back to the Hellenistic era.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: Make products in America or face tariffs
item.description: “Come make your product in America, and we will give you among the lowest taxes of any nation on earth,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: At least 12 train passengers killed in India after jumping onto tracks to flee alleged fire
item.description: In India, at least 12 train passengers died and at least six others were injured when they jumped out of their train cars to escape a rumored fire and were struck by another train.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tabloids apologize to Prince Harry, admit intruding on Princess Diana
item.description: Prince Harry claimed a monumental victory Wednesday as Rupert Murdoch’s U.K. tabloids made an unprecedented apology.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia warns Trump against seizing Panama Canal
item.description: Russian officials reaffirmed their commitment to the neutrality of the critical trade route and said it expects both America and Panama to continue respecting that agreement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation
